instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertion on the 6 Ratios to Consider When Buying Insurance - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Meta Description: While picking an insurer, your choice may be based on the claim settlement ratio. This gives you significant information about the insurance company.
Keywords: claim settlement ratio, persistency ratio, term plan, claim settlement process, combined ratio
Body Content:
When you buy an insurance policy, what do you look for? There are many factors to consider while buying insurance. Moreover, the factors come into play more significantly when you decide on the kind of insurance you want to buy, that is, car insurance, health insurance, life insurance, etc. Choosing a reputed insurance company is a concern for many individuals. You may ask friends for recommendations, or study different policies’ terms and conditions extensively. Furthermore, the premiums you have to pay will influence your purchase decisions.
While deciding what insurance company to sign up with, an aspect of note is the claim settlement ratio. This can give you a measure of the quality of the insurance company you are buying a policy from. From the point of view of a potential policyholder, the reputation of an insurance company plays a large role in the decision-making process of buying a policy. If an insurance company has a history of high claim settlement ratios, the greater the chances of settling claims are. Understanding something about the various ratios in a policy helps you in your policy choice.
Persistency Ratio
The persistency ratio of any insurance policy gives you an idea of how persistent policyholders have been while renewing their policies on a yearly basis. The persistency ratio is measured at intervals. These intervals are at the 13th month, the 25th month, the 37th month and the 61st month. The persistency ratio can tell you a lot about the trust that customers display in the services and long-term products that an insurer offers. Consequently, the persistency ratio gives you an indication of customer satisfaction, with high ratios indicating a lot of customers who are satisfied.  The high persistency ratio implies that the insurance company is highly rated by customers in all aspects of insurance, including after-sales service.
Solvency Ratio
The solvency ratio represents the financial condition of the insurance company. This is defined by norms that are solvency specific. Based on guidelines by IRDAI, all insurance companies must have a solvency ratio amounting to 150%. This is maintained to reduce the risk of bankruptcy. The ratio is a good indicator of a company’s ability to undergo a claim settlement process with a sufficient buffer in extreme circumstances. If the solvency ratio is high, the company has enough wealth to tackle any liabilities, both in the short run and in the long run. Moreover, such a company will be more likely to settle your claims easily.
Combined Ratio
This relates to a general insurance company and its entire outflow of cash. The outflow refers to expenses for operation, paid commissions, and incurred losses and claims on its earned net premium. A company that has a low combined ratio is one in which losses or expenses are less than its revenue generated by premiums for a particular period. This means less cash outflow compared to premiums collected. Such a company is a good candidate to purchase an insurance policy from. Nonetheless, a high combined ratio does not necessarily mean that the insurance company is incurring losses, because a combined ratio does not consider investment earnings.
Incurred Claims Ratio
This metric shows the ability of a general insurer to settle claims. For example, an incurred claims ratio equal to 85%, indicates that the insurer has spent an amount of 85 on claims for each Rs. 100 in premium collection. A healthy incurred claims ratio should be in the range of 75% - 90%.
Commission Expense Ratio
When you wish to know the outflow towards the commission from the premiums written in a specific period, you can look at the commission expense ratio. This is one ratio to keep a track of as it has a direct impact on the premiums you pay. A lower ratio means lower premiums, as more discounts can be offered.
Claim Settlement Ratio
The claim settlement ratio is viewed before you buy any kind of insurance plan. Indicative of the number of claims that an insurance company has settled compared to the number of received claims. This is the chief ratio to view when you are picking out an insurance plan, like a health plan. For some policyholders,’this is the key deciding factor in choosing a plan, as it is indicative of the insurers reputation. The claim settlement ratio is especially important when you purchase a pure life insurance policy like a term plan.
Conclusion
If you are thinking of buying insurance plans, it's a good idea to look at ratios when you buy health insurance and vehicle insurance. Besides these ratios, for life insurance plans, other factors may be important to account for when you buy plans. These Include cost, benefits guaranteed, portfolio, tenure, returns, etc, especially for combined investment and savings plans. Before you make a purchase decision, be sure of other things like customer service, terms, and conditions, etc, of a plan.
input.compliance_comments:
[Harshita Rajawat]: claim settlement ratio (5) – 0.6%persistency ratio (6) – 0.7%term plan (1) – 0.1%claim settlement process (1) – 0.1%combined ratio (4) – 0.4%The Total Keyword Density is 1.9% which is ideal for 812 words count article.The placements of the target keywords are apt. (Context: SEO Insertion on the 6 Ratios to Consider When Buying Insurance - Article Page)
[Harshita Rajawat]: Please replace the term with “Term Insurance” (Context: The claim settlement ratio is viewed before you buy any kind of insurance plan. ...)
